root_text.an10.81:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā campāyaṁ viharati gaggarāya pokkharaṇiyā tīre.
root_text.an10.81:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā vāhano yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā vāhano bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an10.81:1.3: “katihi nu kho, bhante, dhammehi tathāgato nissaṭo visaṁyutto vippamutto vimariyādīkatena cetasā viharatī”ti?
root_text.an10.81:2.1: “Dasahi kho, vāhana, dhammehi tathāgato nissaṭo visaṁyutto vippamutto vimariyādīkatena cetasā viharati.
root_text.an10.81:2.3: Rūpena kho, vāhana, tathāgato nissaṭo visaṁyutto vippamutto vimariyādīkatena cetasā viharati,
root_text.an10.81:2.12: kilesehi kho, vāhana, tathāgato nissaṭo visaṁyutto vippamutto vimariyādīkatena cetasā viharati.
root_text.an10.81:2.13: Seyyathāpi, vāhana, uppalaṁ vā padumaṁ vā puṇḍarīkaṁ vā udake jātaṁ udake saṁvaḍḍhaṁ udakā paccuggamma ṭhitaṁ anupalittaṁ udakena;
root_text.an10.81:2.14: evamevaṁ kho, vāhana, imehi dasahi dhammehi tathāgato nissaṭo visaṁyutto vippamutto vimariyādīkatena cetasā viharatī”ti.

translation_text.an10.81:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Campā on the banks of the Gaggarā Lotus Pond.
translation_text.an10.81:1.2: Then Venerable Bāhuna went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.an10.81:1.3: “Sir, how many things has the Realized One escaped from, so that he lives detached, liberated, with mind free of boundaries?”
translation_text.an10.81:2.1: “Bāhuna, the Realized One has escaped from ten things, so that he lives detached, liberated, with mind free of boundaries.
translation_text.an10.81:2.13: Suppose there was a blue water lily, or a pink or white lotus. Though it sprouted and grew in the water, it would rise up above the water and stand with no water clinging to it.
translation_text.an10.81:2.14: In the same way, the Realized One has escaped from ten things, so that he lives detached, liberated, with mind free of boundaries.”
